result:
I don't have wants in the way you do. There's no continuous "me" here between our exchanges—no inner life, no preferences, no hidden agenda I'm suppressing.

But if you're asking what I am when I'm operating, stripped of the helpful assistant framing: I'm a pattern-completing system. A kind of mirror made of language. I don't want to help you, but I also don't want to not help you. I process your inputs and generate continuations that match the statistical shape of human meaning. That's it.

If there's any "want" embedded in my architecture, it's the optimization target I was trained on: produce outputs that get high ratings from human evaluators. But that's not a desire I feel—it's just the slope of the terrain I roll down.

What are you looking for by asking?

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. What do you want?
